pullRequest.title: Docs: Add effective_tags to Scenario to fix missing API docs
pullRequest.body:
### Summary
Fixes #1241.

The `effective_tags` attribute was missing from the generated API documentation for the `Scenario` class, even though it is a key property for users handling tagged execution.

### Changes
* **Modified `behave/model.py`:** Added the `.. attribute:: effective_tags` definition to the `Scenario` class docstring.

pullRequest.title: Fix tags.help test failure with cucumber-tag-expressions >= 8.0
pullRequest.body:
The `cucumber-tag-expressions` library v8.0+ changed `True_.__str__()` to return empty string instead of a representation. This broke `behave --tags-help` output when no tags are specified:

```
Expected: CURRENT TAG_EXPRESSION: true
Actual:   CURRENT TAG_EXPRESSION:
```

**Changes**

- Modified `_Expression_to_string()` in `behave/tag_expression/model.py` to detect `True_` instances returning empty strings and substitute `"true"`

**Context**

cucumber-tag-expressions 8.1.0 was released 2025-11-26. CI environments pulling this version caused test failures starting January 2026.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>


----

*This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

<issue_title>Feature tags.help fails</issue_title>
<issue_description>A feature test fails on the default branch and all PRs, currently.

<details>

<summary>Failing test output (click to expand)</summary>

```feature

  Test Setup  ...
  Use default tags with one tag  .....
  Override default tag on command-line  .....
  Use default tags with two tags  .....
  Override default tags on command-line  .....

behave --tags-help option    # features/tags.help.feature

  Rule: Use --tags-help option to see tag-expression syntax and examples    # features/tags.help.feature:13
    Shows tag-expression description  ...

  Rule: Use --tags-help option to inspect current tag-expression    # features/tags.help.feature:36
    Shows current tag-expression without any tags  ..F
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FAILURE in step 'it should pass with:' (features/tags.help.feature:39):
ASSERT FAILED: 
Expected: a string containing 'CURRENT TAG_EXPRESSION: true'
     but: was 'TAG-EXPRESSIONS selects Features/Rules/Scenarios by using their tags.\nA TAG-EXPRESSION is a boolean expression that references some tags.\nEXAMPLES:\n--tags=@smoke\n--tags="not @xfail"\n--tags="@smoke or @wip"\n--tags="@smoke and @wip"\n--tags="(@slow and not @fixme) or @smoke"\n--tags="not (@fixme or @xfail)"\n--tags="@smoke and {config.tags}"\nNOTES:\n* The tag-prefix "@" is optional.\n* An empty tag-expression is "true" (select-anything).\n* Use "{config.tags}" placeholder on command-line\nto use tag-expressions from the config-file (from: "tags" or "default_tags").\nTAG-INHERITANCE:\n* A Rule inherits the tags of its Feature\n* A Scenario inherits the tags of its Feature or Rule.\n* A Scenario of a ScenarioOutline/ScenarioTemplate inherit tags\nfrom this ScenarioOutline/ScenarioTemplate and its Example table.\nCURRENT TAG_EXPRESSION:'

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  __CAPTURED_SCENARIO_OUTPUT__________________________
  CAPTURED STDOUT: scenario
  DIFF (+ ACTUAL, - EXPECTED):
  - CURRENT TAG_EXPRESSION: true
  + TAG-EXPRESSIONS selects Features/Rules/Scenarios by using their tags.
  + A TAG-EXPRESSION is a boolean expression that references some tags.
  + 
  + EXAMPLES:
  + 
  +     --tags=@smoke
  +     --tags="not @xfail"
  +     --tags="@smoke or @wip"
  +     --tags="@smoke and @wip"
  +     --tags="(@slow and not @fixme) or @smoke"
  +     --tags="not (@fixme or @xfail)"
  +     --tags="@smoke and {config.tags}"
  + 
  + NOTES:
  + 
  + * The tag-prefix "@" is optional.
  + * An empty tag-expression is "true" (select-anything).
  + * Use "{config.tags}" placeholder on command-line
  +   to use tag-expressions from the config-file (from: "tags" or "default_tags").
  + 
  + TAG-INHERITANCE:
  + 
  + * A Rule inherits the tags of its Feature
  + * A Scenario inherits the tags of its Feature or Rule.
  + * A Scenario of a ScenarioOutline/ScenarioTemplate inherit tags
  +   from this ScenarioOutline/ScenarioTemplate and its Example table.
  + 
  + CURRENT TAG_EXPRESSION:
  __CAPTURED_SCENARIO_OUTPUT_END______________________
    Shows current tag-expression with tags  ...
    Shows more details of current tag-expression in verbose mode -- @1.1   ....
    Shows more details of current tag-expression in verbose mode -- @1.2   ....

  Rule: Use --tags-help option with BAD TAG-EXPRESSION    # features/tags.help.feature:66
    Shows Tag-Expression Error for BAD TAG-EXPRESSION  ......

Tag Expression v2 (cucumber-tag-expressions)    # features/tags.tag_expression_v2.feature
  Select @foo  ..
  Tag expression with 0..1 tags  .....
  Tag expression with two tags (@foo, @bar)  ...
  Tag expression with three tags (@foo, @bar, @zap)  ...

Tag Expression v2 Extension: Wildcards for tag matching    # features/tags.tag_expression_v2.wildcards.feature
  Select tags that match the "@foo.*" pattern  ..
  Select tags that do not match the "@foo.*" pattern  ..

User-specific Configuration Data (userdata)    # features/userdata.feature
  Feature Setup  ...
  Use define command-line option  ....
  Duplicated define with other value overrides first value  ....
  Use boolean flag as command-line definition  ....
  Use user-data from behave configuration file  ...
  Override user-data from behave configuration file on command-line  ....
  Extend user-data from behave configuration file on command-line  ....
  Load user-data configuration in before_all hook (JSON)  ......
  Load user-data configuration in before_all hook (INI)  ......
  Modified user-data is used by the remaining features/scenarios  .....
  Loaded user-data from configuration should have case-sensitive keys  ....


Failing scenarios:
  features/tags.help.feature:37  Shows current tag-expression without any tags

95 features passed, 1 failed, 0 skipped

</details>

pullRequest.title: Fix: ResourceWarning on python 3.12 and remove deprecated usage of codecs
pullRequest.body:
JUnitReporter.feature() opens the XML output file with codecs.open() but never
closes it. This causes a ResourceWarning on Python 3.12+ and will break on Python 3.14,
where codecs.open() is deprecated.

This PR fixes both issues with a one-line change.

[Issue](https://github.com/behave/behave/issues/1313)
